sanskrit:
लोभः प्रवृत्तिरारम्भः कर्मणामशमः स्पृहा।
रजस्येतानि जायन्ते विवृद्धे भरतर्षभ।।
transliteration:
lobhaḥ pravṛttir ārambhaḥ karmaṇām aśamaḥ spṛhā
rajasy etāni jāyante vivṛddhe bharatarṣabha
literal_meaning: Greed, restlessness, undertaking of actions, inability to rest, craving—these arise when rajas predominates, O best of Bharatas.
contextual_meaning: When the mind is hijacked by the energy of passion, it becomes a treadmill of endless wanting: more status, more stimulation, more achievements. Satisfaction is always just one more goal away, so the person stays agitated and perpetually busy.
psychological_state: Hyper-aroused, compulsive striving driven by an inner sense of insufficiency; classic precursor to anxiety disorders and workaholism.
trigger_scenario: Checking your phone at 2 a.m. for stock/crypto prices, feeling you must buy more even though you already lost sleep and money.
anchor_text: Notice the itch of 'more' and pause before the next chase.
